title: Run QLoRA for low-resource fine-tuning
description: Fine-tune a quantized base with low-rank adapters to fit limited GPU memory while preserving quality.
body: Use when GPU memory cannot hold a full-precision base for tuning. Do not full-fine-tune in fp16 on undersized hardware — it OOMs or forces tiny batches that destabilize training. Actions: load the base quantized (e.g. int4 via bitsandbytes), attach LoRA adapters in higher precision, tune adapters only, then evaluate the dequantized-plus-adapter model against the base. Validation signals include a run that fits the memory budget and a task metric matching a full-precision LoRA baseline within tolerance. Risk boundary: low-bit base quantization can cap achievable quality; if the QLoRA result lags the budget, escalate to more memory or higher precision before shipping.
